w:r.w:t: Test Cases - Task 26464: Make login session carry across all sub domains
Feature: Make changes so login session carries across all sub domains. PR: share cookies across domains.
TS-001 — Basic cross-subdomain login persistence (Happy path)
Description: Verify that after logging in on the main domain, the user remains authenticated when navigating to other subdomains under the same parent domain.
Preconditions: Test account exists; browser cache & cookies cleared; environment accessible via HTTPS; cookie Domain configured to .pgl.co.uk.

Steps: 1) Open fresh browser. 2) Go to https://www.pgl.co.uk/login and sign in. 3) Confirm login on www. 4) Go to https://holiday.pgl.co.uk and confirm authenticated. 5) Inspect cookie Domain=.pgl.co.uk.
Expected: User remains authenticated across subdomains; session cookie set on parent domain.
TS-002 — Login then redirect to subdomain: Validate post-auth redirect retains session.
Steps: Login on www, trigger redirect to https://services.pgl.co.uk/dashboard, verify authenticated landing.
TS-003 — Cookie Secure flag and HTTP vs HTTPS: Secure cookies must not be sent over HTTP.
TS-004 — Cookie Domain attribute correctness: Domain=.pgl.co.uk visible to all subdomains; explicit subdomain cookie is not.
TS-005 — SameSite attribute tests: None vs Lax vs Strict for cross-site contexts (iframes, cross-origin redirects).
TS-006 — Logout propagation across subdomains: Logout on one subdomain invalidates session on all.
TS-007 — Session expiry and cookie expiry boundary: Short timeout test and re-authentication required post-expiry.
TS-008 — Concurrent sessions and session fixation: Attempt cookie reuse in another browser; verify server-side protections.
TS-009 — Cookie header size and server/proxy limits: Large cookie payloads causing truncation or 431 errors.
TS-010 — Subdomain permutations: Test www, regional (en, fr), holiday, services subdomains.
TS-011 — Different ports on same host: Verify cookie/send behavior across ports.
TS-012 — Third-party cookie blocking & ITP: Test in privacy-focused browsers and document fallback behavior.
